page: Amazon Web Services m5d.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8175M processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system includes 192 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is supported by 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store, and network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data places this instance consistently in the average tier for general compute and memory operations. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core Geekbench score of 1148.0 and a multi-core score of 10551.0, with a Passmark CPU score of 34393.15. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only large block speed of 107.02 GB/sec and a write speed of 46.35 GB/sec. However, single-threaded gzip compression is a relative bottleneck, performing in the lower 25% tier at 23.59 GB/sec.

The inclusion of 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside 192 GB of RAM makes the m5d.12xlarge highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space or caching. It is well-suited for database operations, as evidenced by its Passmark database score of 15429.85 and Redis SET throughput of 38.6 million operations per second. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale LLM inference, though large-scale 70B parameter models show weak performance due to the lack of GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8175M processor at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 192 GB of DDR4 RAM and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the Nitro hypervisor with a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate solid, mid-tier capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 34393.15 and a Redis SET throughput of 38.6 million operations per second. While single-threaded compression tasks represent a relative performance bottleneck, the instance delivers reliable memory bandwidth, reaching 107.02 GB/sec for large-block reads. This hardware profile is highly effective for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and applications requiring high-speed local storage caching.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage powered by an Intel Xeon processor on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1800 GB NVMe SSD local storage on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon compute, DDR4 memory, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores of Intel Xeon 8175M at 3.1 GHz
Equipped with 192 GB of DDR4 RAM and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Achieves 38.6 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for databases, web serving, and software compilation workloads
